rag_rules: Insert AGREE annexes into the target component implementation scope when bindings depend on subcomponent ports or implementation-level state.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structure by only inserting annexes into scopes that logically own the referenced ports or properties.
Do not reference AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions unless they are explicitly exposed as typed variables or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax.
Component type annexes are for abstract assumptions/guarantees; implementation annexes are for concrete bindings.
anti_patterns: Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type annexes without intermediate variable declarations.
Never convert Boolean or enumeration ports into numeric range comparisons unless the AADL model explicitly defines them as numeric scalars.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
